title: NexCMS
tagline: A guided website builder for the restaurant and hospitality industry.
description: NexCMS walks restaurant owners, food truck operators, bars, and cafés through every step of building a professional website — business info, hours, menu, branding, social media — and produces a fully deployable, SEO-optimized site. No developer required.
story: Built because restaurant owners shouldn't need a developer to have a great website. Every existing option is either too expensive, too generic, or too technical. NexCMS fills that gap with hospitality-first templates, a coaching wizard, and full export ownership.
journey: NexCMS started as a headless CMS experiment and fully pivoted once the real problem became clear: it's not that restaurant owners lack a CMS, it's that every website builder treats them like a generic small business. The rebuild focused entirely on hospitality — food truck schedules, menu builders, delivery platform links, and a mobile-first SaaS dashboard designed for owners who run their business from their phone.
forWho: Restaurant owners, food truck operators, bar and café owners, and hospitality businesses who want a professional website without hiring a developer or paying monthly SaaS fees.

detailedSpecs.architecture: Turborepo monorepo with pnpm workspaces. Astro 5 hybrid mode for SaaS. React 19 + Vite for the local builder wizard. Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Storage) for the SaaS backend. Single Astro server instance on Railway serving all tenant sites via domain resolution. Cloudflare edge caching with cache purge on content save — live edits in under 10 seconds.
modes: Local Builder — CLI launches a wizard at localhost:3000, exports a deployable Astro zip with deploy instructions
SaaS Hub — nexcms.io, fully in-browser, mobile-first, live editing without rebuild
keyFeatures: Guided onboarding wizard — coached step-by-step, not a blank form
Two-axis template system — business type + visual style chosen independently
SEO auto-generation — meta tags, sitemap, robots.txt, Schema.org
OG image generation via satori + sharp
Menu builder with categories, prices, photos, and dietary tags
Delivery platform links (DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub)
Live editing without rebuild in SaaS mode
Full Astro source export — no vendor lock-in
roadmap: Phase 0 — Planning (Now → Jul 2026): Lock types, template spec, Supabase schema, monorepo scaffold
Phase 1 — Generator Core (Jul → Sep 2026): JSON in → Astro site out
Phase 2 — Local Builder (Sep → Nov 2026): Full wizard UI, all 8 business types, all 6 styles
Phase 3 — SaaS Foundation (Nov 2026 → Feb 2027): Supabase, account system, live editing
Phase 4 — SaaS Polish (Feb → Apr 2027): GitHub push, one-click deploy, analytics
Phase 5 — Public Launch (Q2 2027): nexcms.io public, pricing, template marketplace
detailedSpecs.deployTarget: Railway (SaaS server) + Netlify / Vercel / Cloudflare Pages / GitHub (exported local sites)
